ops.title: cc-transcript sibling-drift saga: v10 red resolved as 12.1.0 (2026-07-12); RECURRED 2026-07-14 as v13 drift (daemon.py TailState gone, stale Rust .so) owned by unmerged cc66ac9; watcher launchd-supervised

ops.body:
**Resolved 2026-07-12.** The chronic cc-steer CI red (5 test modules failing at
collection on `ImportError: sample_windows`) was NOT an upstream API drop: the
symbols (`cc_transcript.mining.sampling`, `cc_transcript.watch`) had **never been
committed** — they existed only as uncommitted WIP in the local `~/Code/cc-transcript`
working copy, which cc-steer's `cd272da` was co-developed against. Fix: the WIP's
dependency slice was landed upstream (`a8cdf46` mining.sampling, `467017b` watch +
CLI) and released as **cc-transcript 12.1.0**; cc-steer's pin bumped to
`>=12.1,<13` (`2b51cfd`). cc-steer main CI is fully green (pytest + wheel smoke +
Linux ty) as of 2026-07-12.

**Why:** "CI red = upstream drift" was a mis-diagnosis that survived two agents;
the truth (unshipped local WIP) only surfaced by checking `git log --all` /
`git ls-tree origin/main` for the missing symbols. Also: uv does NOT enforce
version specifiers against `tool.uv.sources` path overrides, so a constraint bump
is locally safe even when the sibling checkout's version is older.

**How to apply:** When an import exists locally but not in CI, check whether it
was ever committed upstream before planning a "migration". Residual: the
`~/Code/cc-transcript` working copy still carries un-landed WIP (facts/tools/
judge/store/render edits) on a stale detached HEAD — `jj rebase` onto main will
no-op the landed files. The shadow watcher remains supervised under launchd:
`com.cc-steer.watch` (KeepAlive+RunAtLoad) via `cc_steer/launchd.py` +
`cc-steer pipeline install-launchd --watch`; restart/promotion =
`launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.cc-steer.watch`. The Jul 2026 "watcher
stall" was a false alarm (gate selectivity), see [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]].
